quick_facts.label: Entry type
quick_facts.value: Army Cadet College (ACC) is listed by the Ministry of Defence’s parliamentary material as a service entry to commissioned-officer training, rather than a UPSC civilian examination.

quick_facts.label: Published baseline eligibility
quick_facts.value: A Ministry of Defence parliamentary reference lists ACC as requiring 10+2 qualification and an age range of 20–27 years. These are published reference criteria, not a substitute for the current Army order or course notification.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: The published service-entry process specifies Army personality-development training and a written entrance examination before a five-day Service Selection Board (SSB) assessment; recommended candidates then proceed under the applicable medical and merit processes.

quick_facts.label: How applications move
quick_facts.value: Applications for service entries are processed through the candidate’s unit or Formation Headquarters, Records office, and personnel/line directorates—not through the ordinary civilian UPSC application route.

quick_facts.label: Training pathway
quick_facts.value: The Ministry of Defence parliamentary table describes the ACC course as 3+1 years and identifies selection as an Army Headquarters written examination plus SSB; course design, vacancies, dates, and detailed conditions can change by Army instructions.

sections.heading: Purpose and place in the officer-selection system
paragraphs: ACC is an in-service route intended to develop eligible serving personnel into commissioned officers. Ministry of Defence material groups it with other service entries, distinct from UPSC entries such as NDA and CDS and from non-UPSC civilian entries. The examination is therefore only one gate in an internal Army selection-and-training pathway, not a stand-alone public university entrance test.

The official parliamentary record identifies the deciding elements as an Army Headquarters written examination and SSB assessment. This makes ACC relevant to serving personnel who seek a permanent-commission officer-training route through Army channels.
bullets: Treat ACC as an internal service-entry selection route, not as a UPSC examination.

Confirm the current course instruction through the unit chain of command, because published historical tables do not establish current vacancies, dates, medical standards, number of attempts, or branch-specific conditions.

sections.heading: Eligibility: use the current Army order as controlling
paragraphs: The published Ministry of Defence table gives a useful baseline: ACC is shown as a service entry with 10+2 qualification and an age band of 20–27 years. A later Lok Sabha reply separately lists ACC (from Other Ranks) with the same 20–27 age band. These sources support the broad profile but are not a current notification for a particular course.

Eligibility must also be established within the service system. The published process says that ACC applications are processed through units/Formations, Records, and personnel or line directorates, and that processing follows successful Army personality-development training and qualification in the written entrance examination. Serving personnel should obtain the current eligibility certificate, documentation requirements, medical-category rules, service requirements, and timing from their unit/Record office.
bullets: Do not assume that historic eligibility tables settle current eligibility; verify the course-specific Army instruction before applying.

sections.heading: Assessment format
paragraphs: The official parliamentary account describes ACC selection as sequential: eligible personnel must successfully attend Army-run Personality Development Training and qualify the written entrance examination; SSB interviews are then conducted over five days. The SSB team comprises an Interviewing Officer, Psychologist, and Group Testing Officer.

The same source describes the SSB sequence as Stage I screening through Officer Intelligence Rating and Picture Perception and Description Tests, followed for recommended candidates by interview, group testing, and psychological tests. Candidates should rely on their current call-up instructions for exact reporting arrangements and medical procedures.
bullets: Written entrance examination administered under Army Headquarters arrangements.
Army Personality Development Training before ACC application processing, as described in the published process.
Five-day SSB assessment after the qualifying stages.

sections.heading: Application process
paragraphs: ACC is administered through the service chain. The Ministry of Defence’s published procedure states that service-entry applications move from the concerned unit or Formation Headquarters through the relevant Records office and personnel/line directorate. This is materially different from applying directly to UPSC for CDS or NDA.

A practical sequence is to monitor unit/Record-office instructions; confirm current eligibility and documentation; complete the Army-directed preparatory requirement; sit the written entrance examination; and follow official instructions for SSB call-up, medical examination, and final selection. The National Portal of India also directs users seeking Indian Army recruitment forms and instructions—including ACC material—to the official Join Indian Army site.
bullets: Start with the unit administration/personnel channel rather than an external coaching or third-party website.
Keep educational, service, identity, and other documents in the form specified by the current Army instruction.
Use Join Indian Army only as an official information/form source; the controlling process for a serving candidate remains the service chain.

sections.heading: Preparation priorities
paragraphs: Preparation should address both gates of selection. First, prepare for the Army’s written entrance examination in accordance with the current syllabus or unit-issued instructions. Second, prepare for the multi-assessor SSB process: the published SSB structure includes intelligence and perception screening, followed by interview, group testing, and psychological assessment for those who clear Stage I.

The Army has institutional support for prospective service-entry candidates: a Lok Sabha reply states that the Young Leaders Training Wing at Officers’ Training Academy, Chennai was established in September 2019 to groom prospective candidates for in-service entries including ACC. Candidates should use officially allotted Army training and unit guidance rather than treating private coaching claims as authoritative.
bullets: Obtain the current written-exam syllabus from official/unit channels before choosing study material.
Practise clear communication, group participation, reasoning, and self-reflection for the SSB assessment stages described in the Ministry of Defence record.
Follow the conduct, fitness, and administrative directions issued by the unit and current call-up documentation.

sections.heading: Outcome after selection
paragraphs: The intended outcome is entry to pre-commission officer training and, on successful completion of the prescribed selection, medical, training, and merit requirements, commissioning in the Indian Army. The published Ministry of Defence table records ACC as a 3+1-year course and identifies selection through an Army Headquarters written examination and SSB.

That duration is historical published context rather than a current guarantee. Serving applicants should verify the current course structure, commissioning terms, seniority, pay and allowances during training, service liability, and vacancies in the applicable Army order; these matters may be revised by policy or course instructions.
bullets: Successful written-exam performance alone does not confer a commission.
SSB, medical fitness, merit, and completion of prescribed training remain consequential stages.
Do not infer a fixed intake or timetable from older published material.
